adds.rationale: IonQ is the only pure-play US-listed quantum computing stock and fills a complete gap in the watchlist for the quantum wave, which the investor profile names as a high-priority next-wave exposure; the Forte system launch and QuantumBasel deal in late June 2023 drove a 42% July gain and a guidance raise to $45-55M, providing concrete commercial-milestone support for entry.

news_evidence.summary: IonQ stock soared 42% in July 2023 after the company launched its IonQ Forte quantum system for commercial use globally on June 22, signed a partnership with QuantumBasel to establish a European quantum data center, and raised full-year 2023 booking guidance to $45-55M from $38-42M; stock was up over 400% year-to-date as of the article date.

IonQ completed its SPAC merger with dMY Technology Group III and began trading on the NYSE under ticker IONQ on October 1, 2021, becoming the first pure-play quantum computing company listed on a major US exchange; confirms ticker has over two years of price history prior to the 2023-09-30 rebalance date.

IonQ filed its Form 10-Q for the quarter ended June 30, 2023 with the SEC, covering Q2 2023 financial results showing approximately 67% year-over-year revenue growth to $4.35M and confirming the guidance increase; demonstrates active SEC reporting and US-listing compliance.

removes.rationale: China's September 2023 government-worker iPhone ban introduced a material headwind to Apple's single largest geographic growth market (18% of revenue), and with the AI wave bucket already holding four tickers (MSFT, GOOGL, NVDA, AAPL) at the hard per-bucket cap, AAPL is the weakest direct AI thesis of the four and the natural trim to free a slot for quantum coverage.

news_evidence.summary: Apple shares fell more than 3% on September 7 and 4% the prior day after reports emerged that China ordered officials at central government agencies to stop bringing iPhones into the office or using them for work; Greater China accounts for 18% of Apple's total revenue, making this ban a material top-line risk.

NBC News confirmed Apple shares fell on China iPhone ban reports, noting the restrictions on government employees were issued without a public announcement and the full scope of the ban remained unclear, adding regulatory uncertainty on top of the demand headwind.

rationale_overall: The watchlist was at the 8-ticker cap with no quantum computing coverage despite the investor profile naming it an explicit next-wave priority. IonQ's concrete June-July 2023 milestones (Forte system launch, European data center deal, guidance raised to $45-55M) make it the strongest evidence-backed add for that gap. AAPL is removed because the AI wave bucket was at its 4-ticker hard cap and AAPL carries the weakest direct AI thesis among the four AI names; the September 2023 China government iPhone ban added a China-decoupling headwind that further erodes the conviction case for holding it over the other three. Post-change, the watchlist covers six distinct wave buckets — AI (MSFT/GOOGL/NVDA), robotics (BOTZ), rockets/spacecraft (RKLB), quantum (IONQ), geopolitical/defense (ITA), and general markets (SPY) — aligning more tightly with the profile's multi-wave thesis.
